qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Salvo proxy forwards all hop-by-hop headers to upstream servers enabling HTTP request smuggling
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes unbounded memory allocation in Salvo's form data parsing (Request::form_data() in the core crate) leading to OOM denial of service. This finding describes HTTP request smuggling via the salvo-proxy crate forwarding hop-by-hop headers. The vulnerable components differ (core form parser vs. proxy handler), the root causes differ (missing size limits on body reading vs. missing hop-by-hop header filtering), and the vulnerability classes differ (CWE-770 resource exhaustion vs. HTTP request smuggling).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes denial of service via unbounded memory allocation in Salvo's form data parsing (Request::form_data() and Extractible macro reading request bodies without size limits, CWE-770). The only qualifying finding (proxy-header-smuggling) describes a different vulnerability in a different component: HTTP request smuggling via the salvo-proxy crate's failure to filter hop-by-hop headers. These are distinct root causes, distinct vulnerable code paths, and distinct vulnerability classes.
evidence: Advisory root cause: Request::form_data() calls BodyExt::collect(body) reading entire body into memory without size checking (core crate).
Advisory vulnerable component: form data parsing in salvo core, specifically form_data() method and Extractible macro.
Finding root cause: salvo-proxy forwards all headers except HOST to upstream without filtering hop-by-hop headers (proxy crate, lib.rs:420-424).
Finding vulnerable component: salvo-proxy reverse proxy handler (crates/proxy/src/lib.rs, hyper_client.rs, reqwest_client.rs).
No code overlap: the finding's code_locations (crates/proxy/src/) are entirely separate from the advisory's affected component (core form parsing).
